disclaimer: Cannabinoid figures are typical published ranges, not batch values — prescribed products carry batch-specific certificates of analysis. Strain availability under these names varies by clinic formulary. This is educational information, not medical advice; product selection is a decision for your prescribing clinician.

strains.name: Bedrocan
strains.ukAvailability: Standardised pharmaceutical-grade product prescribed in the UK
strains.description: The most widely prescribed standardised flower in Europe. Grown to pharmaceutical GMP standards with batch-consistent cannabinoid content, it is the benchmark high-THC product in many UK prescriptions.

strains.name: Bediol
strains.ukAvailability: Standardised pharmaceutical-grade product prescribed in the UK
strains.description: A balanced THC:CBD product designed for patients who need symptom relief with minimal impairment. Often the starting point for cannabis-naive patients in UK titration plans.

strains.name: Bedrolite
strains.ukAvailability: Standardised pharmaceutical-grade product prescribed in the UK
strains.description: A CBD-dominant flower with negligible THC, prescribed where cannabidiol is the therapeutic target. No meaningful intoxication at standard doses.

strains.name: Bedica
strains.ukAvailability: Standardised pharmaceutical-grade product prescribed in the UK
strains.description: An indica-type standardised flower with a myrcene-forward profile, typically prescribed for evening use where sleep and muscle relaxation are the goals.

strains.name: OG Kush
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: One of the most influential cultivars in modern cannabis, parent to much of the 'Kush' and 'Cookies' families. High-THC with a heavy, relaxing finish.

strains.name: Blue Dream
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: A sativa-leaning hybrid famous for balancing cerebral lift with gentle body relaxation — a daytime-friendly profile at moderate doses.

strains.name: Northern Lights
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: A foundational indica known for fast-onset physical relaxation. Classic evening cultivar referenced by many modern indica lines.

strains.name: White Widow
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: A 1990s Dutch classic with a balanced but potent effect profile; the parent of an extended 'White' family of cultivars.

strains.name: Sour Diesel
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: A fast-acting cerebral sativa with the signature fuel aroma. Popular for daytime symptom management where sedation is unwanted.

strains.name: Granddaddy Purple
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: A deeply physical indica with a grape-forward terpene profile, typically chosen for end-of-day pain and sleep support.

strains.name: Jack Herer
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: The cultivar behind Bedrocan's flagship product — a clear-headed, functional sativa profile that defined an entire category.

strains.name: GG4 (Gorilla Glue #4)
strains.lineage: Chem's Sister × Sour Dubb × Chocolate Diesel
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: A very high-THC hybrid with heavy resin production and a strongly physical effect; common in high-potency UK flower categories under various names.

strains.name: GSC
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: Parent of the 'Cookies' and 'Cake' families that dominate modern menus. Potent, dessert-leaning profile with strong appetite effects.

strains.name: Gelato
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: A dessert-profile hybrid widely grown for the regulated European market; balanced effects with a pronounced sweet terpene profile.

strains.name: Wedding Cake
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: A potent indica-leaning hybrid common in EU-GMP supply chains; rich, doughy terpene profile with a heavy finish.

strains.name: Amnesia Haze
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: A European coffee-shop staple with a long-lasting cerebral effect. Strong daytime profile; higher doses can feel racy for anxiety-prone patients.

strains.name: Super Lemon Haze
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: A multi-award-winning citrus sativa; bright, functional, and strongly limonene-forward.

strains.name: Durban Poison
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: A pure landrace sativa prized for clean, alert energy with little body load — one of the most functional daytime profiles.

strains.name: Purple Punch
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: A dessert indica bred for evening use; grape candy profile and a fast, heavy wind-down.

strains.name: Zkittlez
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: A moderate-THC indica with an unusually fruity terpene profile — often better tolerated by patients sensitive to very high potency.

strains.name: Pink Kush
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: A heavily physical OG descendant common in Canadian-supplied UK menus; strong analgesic reputation among patients.

strains.name: Critical
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: A workhorse European indica found across EU-GMP producers; dependable, moderate-to-strong relaxation.

strains.name: Tangie
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: A citrus-dominant sativa whose terpene profile (high limonene) is often cited in mood-focused product selection.

strains.name: Skunk #1
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: The 1970s hybrid that stabilised modern cannabis breeding. Moderate potency by today's standards; historically significant.

strains.name: Cannatonic
strains.ukAvailability: Available via UK clinic formularies (product names vary by producer)
strains.description: One of the first widely available CBD-rich cultivars; a near-balanced ratio favoured where THC sensitivity is a concern.

strains.name: Harlequin
strains.ukAvailability: Available via UK clinic formularies (product names vary by producer)
strains.description: A reliably CBD-dominant sativa (roughly 5:2 CBD:THC) used for daytime pain and anxiety management with minimal impairment.

strains.name: ACDC
strains.ukAvailability: Available via UK clinic formularies (product names vary by producer)
strains.description: Among the highest-CBD flower cultivars in circulation, with CBD:THC ratios up to 20:1. Effectively non-intoxicating at typical doses.

strains.name: Charlotte's Web
strains.ukAvailability: Available via UK clinic formularies (product names vary by producer)
strains.description: The cultivar that changed the public conversation on paediatric epilepsy and CBD. Negligible THC; CBD-led therapeutic profile.

strains.name: Do-Si-Dos
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: A very potent Cookies-family indica increasingly common in EU-GMP supply; strong evening/sleep profile.

strains.name: MAC 1
strains.ukAvailability: Cultivar name common internationally — check current UK formularies for equivalents
strains.description: A modern connoisseur hybrid with a balanced but strong effect and a distinctive creamy-citrus profile; appears in premium UK menus.
